channel.title: Find Your Ink
channel.description: Christ-centered healing, emotional transformation, and spiritual depth. Written from scars, not wounds — by David Adesina.

item.title: Why Smart Christians Struggle to Feel Their Feelings
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: We were taught to think our way to God. To reason through the pain. To exegete our way out of the darkness. But feelings don't respond to arguments — and the part of you that needs healing isn't the part that reads commentaries.
item.content:encoded:
I used to argue with my own sadness.

Not out loud. In the interior courtroom where I spent most of my prayer life — I would cross-examine the feeling. Build a case against it. Present evidence for why it wasn't warranted, why it wasn't proportional, why a man who knew Romans 8:28 had no business sitting in this particular darkness.

I thought I was being disciplined. What I was actually doing was abandoning myself.

Read the full essay at Find Your Ink.

item.title: The Sabbath Your Ancestors Never Took
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Generational rest debt is real. You're tired in ways sleep can't fix — because the exhaustion didn't begin with you.

item.title: The Difference Between Being Anointed and Being Whole
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: God can use broken things. He does it all the time. But he doesn't want you to stay broken — and those two truths need to be held together.

item.title: When Prayer Feels Like Performance
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: The exhaustion of spiritual theater. The relief of praying nothing at all. And what it means when you can't find the words.

item.title: Decolonizing Your Faith
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: What African Christians inherited that isn't biblical — and what we must return to the Giver.

item.title: Rewiring: The Neuroscience of Romans 12:2
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Long before scientists tracked synaptic changes, Scripture declared: be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Renewing — not repaired once, then left to rust.

item.title: The Violence of Unforgiveness
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Unforgiveness isn't just spiritual rebellion. It's a neural prison. Every time you rehearse the wound, you deepen the groove.

item.title: Healing Is Not Linear
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: The Christian healing journey spirals before it rises. Going back to old wounds isn't failure — it's the work going deeper.

item.title: When the Church Hurt You
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: How to hold faith and wounds from the church in the same hands — without losing either one.

item.title: Fatherlessness and the Father
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: What happens when the primary image you have of fatherhood is absence — and the Gospel asks you to call God "Father."

item.title: Lament as Worship
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: The Psalms didn't sanitize grief — they gave it a voice. What the church lost when it stopped lamenting.

item.title: The Stigma of Christian Mental Health
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Inside the church, "just pray harder" has silenced too many people who needed real help. The cost of that silence is generational.

item.title: Rest as Rebellion
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: In a culture that equates exhaustion with faithfulness, Sabbath is not just a command — it is a countercultural act of trust.

item.title: Emotional Triggers Are Not Your Enemy
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Triggers are not proof of failed healing. They are the body's memory — and they hold the map to what still needs attention.

item.title: The Neuroscience of Shame
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Shame isn't just an emotion — it's a full-body neurological event. Here's what Scripture and science say about how to get free.

item.title: Attachment Theory and the Love of God
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Our earliest bonds shape the lens through which we receive love — including divine love. Here's how to heal what blocks the receiving.

item.title: What African Fathers Couldn't Give You
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Between survival, migration, and a culture that equated stoicism with strength — here's what was lost, and how to grieve it without losing them.

item.title: The Comparison Trap
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Comparison is not just envy — it's a theological error. Here's the identity framework that dismantles it from the roots.

item.title: Boundaries Are Not Betrayal
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: For people raised in cultures where saying no was disloyalty, limits feel like abandonment. Here's the theology of healthy limits.

item.title: The Weight of Being First
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: First-generation success carries a grief no one warns you about. Here's what it costs to climb when the ladder wasn't built for you.

item.title: How Childhood Wounds Shape Adult Faith
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: The theology you inherited and the wounds you carry are not separate. Here's how to untangle them without losing your faith or your sanity.

item.title: When Your Faith and Therapy Disagree
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: What to do when your therapist says one thing and your theology says another — and why most conflicts between faith and psychology are not what they appear.

item.title: Spiritual Warfare Is Internal
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Most spiritual warfare isn't fought against external darkness. It's fought against the lies you've believed about yourself for decades.

item.title: What Sanctification Looks Like on a Bad Day
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: The Instagram version of holiness doesn't survive contact with real life. Here's what becoming more like Christ actually looks like in the mess.

item.title: The God Who Meets You in the Waiting
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Waiting is not empty time. It is the season in which God does the work we cannot see — and the thing most likely to break us if we misread it.

item.title: The Body Holds Your Story
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Trauma doesn't just live in memory — it lives in the body. What somatic science and Scripture say about releasing what the flesh still carries.

item.title: Conviction vs. Shame
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: The Holy Spirit convicts. The enemy accuses. Learning to tell the difference is one of the most important skills in the spiritual life.

item.title: Breaking the Silence: Black Church and Mental Health
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: The Black church has given generations a place to survive. Now it must become a place where they can also heal.

item.title: The Theology of Crying
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Tears are not weakness. In Scripture, they are language — the vocabulary of a heart too full for words.

item.title: On Being African and Not Allowed to Fall Apart
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: In many African homes and churches, falling apart is not permitted. Here's what that cost us — and what healing asks us to unlearn.

item.title: When Forgiveness Feels Impossible
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: The problem isn't your faith. It's the version of forgiveness you were handed — the one that skips past grief and calls it obedience.

item.title: The Inner Child God Never Lost
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: While you were learning to survive, God kept the version of you that was made before the damage. Here's how to meet that child again.

item.title: What Is Christian Counseling?
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Christian counseling is not prayer with a couch. Here's what it actually is, how it differs from secular therapy, and who it's for.

item.title: The Problem with Toxic Positivity in the Church
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: When the church performs hope instead of holding pain, it abandons the people who need it most. Scripture models something far more honest.

item.title: How to Hear God When You're Dissociated
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Dissociation cuts you off from your body, your emotions, and often your sense of God's presence. Here's how to find your way back.

item.title: What It Means to Be Known
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Being known is not the same as being seen. Here's what Psalm 139 means by divine knowledge — and why it's the most terrifying and healing truth in Scripture.

item.title: When "I'll Pray for You" Is Not Enough
item.dc:creator: David Adesina
item.description: Prayer is powerful. And it can also be a way to feel faithful while avoiding the cost of showing up. Here's what James 2 says about both.

item.title: The Bane of the Gifted
item.description: It's not pride that haunts the gifted — it's blindness. The familiarity of your own brilliance can become the very thing that blinds you to the purpose behind it.

item.title: Heaven Still Interrupts Boardrooms
item.description: God doesn't place His children in powerful rooms to make them feel small. He places them there to remind the powerful who truly gives wisdom.
